what_it_does: A fast-coverage inventory note cataloguing four subsystem regions of the Company codebase — nodes/, roles/, mcp_face/, and voice/ — listing folder structure, a per-file purpose table with priority ratings, a summary of counts, top-10 deep-read priorities, architecture insights, and legacy/experimental notes.
observations: Frontmatter declares type: research, source: fast-inventory, region covering nodes/+roles/+mcp_face/+voice/, scan_date 2026-06-08, method: coverage (not deep).
Reproduces directory trees for mcp_face/, nodes/ (19 node-types), roles/ (8 role files + AGENTS), and voice/ including ears/, engines/, ops/systemd, models/, ref/.
Inventory table assigns each file a PRIORITY of YES / Maybe / No with a one-line note.
Records concrete operational details: VRAM figures (Canary ~10.06 GB, Granite ~4.66 GB, Parakeet ~3.05 GB, Qwen3-TTS ~6 GB at 2700 tok/s) and port assignments (ears :2031-2033, engines :4124-4128).
States totals: 68 files scanned, 19 node-types (7 core), 9 cognition roles, STT registry + Kokoro TTS + 5 trial engines.

Architecture Insights section names self-registering patterns (C2), fabric guards, swappable registries (PROVIDER_LIST, ENGINE_REGISTRY, ROLE_REGISTRY), a VRAM convergence point (2026-06-06), and universal layers (speakable, Suite).
Uses context/section codes throughout (C2, C2.1-C2.5, C7, S6, G2-G4) to tie files to a numbered framework.
standouts: The VRAM convergence note: voice lifecycle unified VRAM authority with ops/cli/gpu.py (the keeper) on 2026-06-06, closing a dual-authority bug.
The recurring self-registration-by-file-presence pattern applied uniformly to nodes, roles, and voice engines/ears (no central registry).
Concrete measured engine metrics embedded in an inventory note (port + VRAM + throughput).
conventions: Follows the research/inventory frontmatter convention (type, source, region, scan_date, method).
Uses a priority-tagged table (YES/Maybe/No) as the inventory backbone.
References the codebase's numbered context/framework codes (C2, C7, S6, G2-G4) rather than re-explaining them.
notes: A 2026-06-08 coverage-only map of the nodes/roles/mcp_face/voice regions with per-file priorities — a navigation index, not a deep read.
questions: What do the context codes (C2.3, C7, S6, G2-G4) resolve to — where is the framework these reference defined?
Is the 'fast coverage (not deep)' status still accurate, or have deep-reads since superseded this?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is a fast coverage scan and delivers exactly a coverage-level inventory with explicit non-deep caveats.
apparent_themes: Universal/shared layers across UI and agent faces
intention_signals: Aims to give a future deep-reader a prioritized map of four subsystems — what exists, where, how important, and which 10 files to read first — without claiming completeness.
novelty: The uniform application of one self-registration pattern across nodes, roles, and voice engines, surfaced as a cross-region insight from a coverage scan.
contribution: Contributes a prioritized, port/VRAM-annotated map of the nodes/roles/mcp_face/voice subsystems and the cross-cutting patterns (self-registration, fabric guards, swappable catalogs, VRAM convergence) that bind them.
summary_for_embedding: A fast-coverage inventory note mapping the Company codebase's nodes/, roles/, mcp_face/, and voice/ subsystems with per-file priorities, port/VRAM details, and cross-region architecture insights.
intention_for_embedding: To give deep-readers a prioritized navigation map of four cognition/voice subsystems and the patterns connecting them, without asserting deep coverage.
